subject: Mathematics
title: Infinite New Guests
description: A 60-second lesson on Infinite New Guests.

hook.text: An infinitely long bus arrives at the full infinite hotel. Every passenger needs a room.

buildup.text: Move every current guest to double their room number: room 1 to 2, room 2 to 4, room 3 to 6.

discovery.text: All odd-numbered rooms are now empty. Infinite passengers fill rooms 1, 3, 5, 7 and so on.

twist.text: You just fit a countably infinite group into an already full countably infinite hotel.

climax.text: This works because aleph-null plus aleph-null still equals aleph-null.

punchline.text: Double infinity? Still the same infinity.

quiz.question: How are infinitely many new guests accommodated?
options: Move each guest to double their room number, freeing all odd rooms
